serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve portion of Veggie For One is approximately 200 g, representing one plate of mixed vegetables as served in a Singapore hawker or canteen setting.

nutrient_source.title: Veggie For One — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for energy (55 kcal/100g), protein (10.7 g/100g), fat (0.0 g/100g), and carbohydrate (2.36 g/100g) are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database record for 'Veggie For One' (cr_id G00010997). Secondary nutrient values (dietary fibre, sodium, saturated fat, sugar) are estimated from the HPB database range for comparable mixed vegetable dishes in the Singapore Foodo database, as the HPB record does not publish these fields individually for this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed vegetable dishes, low-carbohydrate vegetable analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'Veggie For One' as a Singapore-specific product. GI estimated at 32 based on analogous low-carbohydrate mixed vegetable dishes (non-starchy vegetables, predominantly green vegetables with legume/tofu protein) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Carbohydrate content of 2.36 g/100g is very low; GL per 200g serving = (32 × 4.72) / 100 = 1.5, confirming negligible glycaemic impact.

hypertension.key_factor: Estimated sodium at 280 mg/100g is borderline for a vegetable dish; potassium at 350 mg/100g provides a favourable Na:K ratio supporting blood pressure regulation.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension may consume this dish but should request reduced-salt preparation and avoid additional sauces, as sodium content can vary significantly depending on cooking method and seasoning used in hawker or canteen settings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.36 g/100g), high protein (10.7 g/100g), and estimated dietary fibre (~2.5 g/100g) combine to produce a negligible glycaemic load (GL ~1.5 per serving), making this dish highly suitable for blood glucose management.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate content, high protein, and low glycaemic index (~32); patients should verify that no high-sugar sauces are added during preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat (0.0 g/100g), zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and a plant-based profile with dietary fibre and potassium support cardiovascular health.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The fat-free, plant-dominant composition of this dish is favourable for cardiovascular disease management; however, patients should confirm sodium content with the vendor, as hawker-style preparation may involve variable salt or sauce additions that could elevate sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Plant-based, zero saturated fat, likely rich in antioxidants (vitamin C ~15 mg/100g) and anti-inflammatory phytonutrients from cruciferous vegetables.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory plant-based profile; patients sensitive to nightshade vegetables (tomato, pepper, eggplant) should verify ingredients and request preparation without nightshades if needed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Veggie For One is a single-serve mixed vegetable dish available in Singapore hawker centres and canteen settings, catalogued in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010997). The dish is characterised by a high protein content of 10.7 g per 100g — unusually high for a vegetable dish — suggesting the inclusion of protein-rich plant ingredients such as tofu, edamame, or legumes alongside the mixed vegetables. The carbohydrate content is very low at 2.36 g/100g, and fat is reported as zero (0.0 g/100g), making this one of the most macronutrient-lean vegetable dishes in the Singapore food database. Energy density is low at 55 kcal per 100g. The dish is classified as a customisable mixed dish, meaning the exact vegetable composition may vary by vendor; typical ingredients may include broccoli, carrots, bean sprouts, cabbage, and snap peas, with tofu or legumes providing the protein base. Preparation is typically by stir-frying or steaming with minimal oil. The flavour profile is mild and savoury, with texture varying from crisp (raw or lightly cooked vegetables) to tender (steamed or longer-cooked preparations). Clinically, this dish is highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (GL ~1.5 per serving), hypertension (with sodium monitoring), and cardiovascular disease (zero fat, zero cholesterol). The plant-based, fibre-rich profile also supports gut health and anti-inflammatory dietary patterns relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management. This dish is vegetarian, vegan, halal, and gluten-free by default, subject to preparation method confirmation.

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate) are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with high confidence (0.95 per source). Secondary nutrients (dietary fibre, sodium, sugar, potassium, vitamin C) are estimated from comparable HPB mixed vegetable dish entries and are not individually published for this specific record, reducing overall confidence to medium. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, not a direct measurement for this product.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per the HPB Singapore Foodo database record for Veggie For One (cr_id G00010997); this is a plant-based dish with no added oil reported in the source data — confirmed zero per HPB. saturated_fat_g and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero consistent with the zero fat value. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in the HPB Foodo database for this record. water_g set to -1: moisture content not published in the HPB Foodo database for this entry. calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data not individually published in the HPB Foodo database for this specific mixed vegetable dish record; detailed micronutrient profiling would require laboratory analysis of a standardised preparation. The unusually high protein value (10.7 g/100g) for a vegetable dish is preserved from the HPB source record and likely reflects the inclusion of tofu, edamame, or legumes in the dish composition. Vendors and consumers should verify actual ingredients as this is a customisable dish. Sodium estimate of 280 mg/100g is derived from HPB database ranges for comparable mixed vegetable dishes and should be verified with the specific vendor. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate based on non-starchy mixed vegetable dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this product.
